killerFeatures.name: Profitability X-ray with route + customer context
killerFeatures.why: Only Cadi has all three: services (price, duration, materials), customers (postcode density, access windows, frequency drift) and schedule (actual durations, travel time between jobs, crew wage cost). Competitors show revenue; Cadi shows true £/hr per service per customer per crew - and explains why the Tuesday domestic round loses money once travel and Sarah's NI-loaded wage are factored. Squeegee/Aworka/Jobber literally cannot compute this because they don't own payroll + route + service catalogue in one schema.
killerFeatures.spec: Nightly Sonnet batch joins services x jobs x payroll x customers x route mileage. Outputs per-service margin %, per-customer margin %, and a per-crew/day P&L. Surfaces as: (a) red/amber chip on Service cards, (b) 'real margin £4.20/hr' line on customer detail, (c) Quarter-view causal insight ('Week 7 dip = 2 unprofitable Britannia jobs + Crew C holiday'). One-tap actions: 'Raise price', 'Re-route', 'Retire service', 'Move customer to weekly'. Edge fn ai-profit-xray; caches scores on services.margin_score, customers.margin_score.

killerFeatures.name: Conversational operations bar (NL edit across schedule + customers + services)
killerFeatures.why: Requires a unified intent parser that can resolve 'Mrs Patel' (customers), 'fortnightly oven clean' (services) and 'Thursday morning' (schedule) in one sentence, plus enforce cross-tab constraints (access windows, crew skills, recurrence rules). Every competitor splits these into separate screens; nobody has a single graph of all three. Mirrors the conversational onboarding/ServiceChat voice already shipped, so it feels native not bolted-on.
killerFeatures.spec: One pinned input ('Ask Cadi'). Sonnet receives compact snapshots of all three domains + recent context. Returns {intent, entities, proposedPatch, clarifyingQ?, confidence}. Patches render as diff modals (ScheduleDiff / CustomerDiff / ServiceDiff) before Apply. Handles: 'Move Mrs Patel to Thursday', 'Raise all exteriors 5% and draft the customer email', 'Show lapsed BS7 residentials worth >£500', 'Add Mrs Henderson 2pm Friday regular conservatory £35'. Reuses ServiceChat parser scaffold.

killerFeatures.name: Causal revenue + churn engine ('why is week 7 down')
killerFeatures.why: Joins free-text customer notes ('mentioned baby due', 'house on market') with schedule cancellations, weather, staff holiday, payroll cost, and service margin to give a narrated cause-of-revenue-change. Rules engines (everyone else) can't read notes; pure ML can't explain. Sonnet over the joined graph does both. This is the 'oh shit' demo moment - turns the vanity 13-week bar chart into a coachable answer and pre-empts churn before the customer ghosts.
killerFeatures.spec: Nightly job scores each customer (churn_score + churn_reason) and each week (revenue_delta_reason). Surfaces as: (a) red chip on customer card, (b) InsightCard under Quarter bar chart, (c) auto-generated win-back batch grouped by lapse-cause with personalised drafts. Edge fns ai-churn-score + ai-quarter-insights. New columns customers.churn_score/reason/scored_at.

commandBar.summary: A single pinned input at the top of the app ('Ask Cadi...') that accepts free-text or voice. Sonnet classifies intent into one of ~10 verbs (schedule.edit, schedule.fill, schedule.optimise, customer.find, customer.add, customer.message, service.price, service.add, service.bundle, insight.explain), extracts entities by name-matching against live customers/services/staff snapshots, and returns a structured patch + human summary. Every action renders as a diff/preview modal with Apply/Undo - never a blind write. Falls back to a clarifying question when confidence <0.7. Same component reused on every tab; tab context is passed as a hint to bias routing.
exampleUtterances: Move Mrs Patel to Thursday and swap Jake and Mel after 1pm
Fill Crew B's gap tomorrow morning from the round backlog
Show me lapsed residentials in BS7 worth more than £500
Add Mrs Henderson, 22 Oak Lane, fortnightly conservatory £35, key in vault
Raise all my exterior services 5% and draft the customer email
Win back everyone I've not seen in 90 days
Optimise tomorrow's route for Crew C, keep Mrs Patel's 10-12 window
Check my prices against the LS postcode market
Wrap up today - push invoices and log mileage
commandBar.routingLogic: Step 1 (Haiku, ~200 tokens): classify intent + extract entity strings. Step 2: resolve entities against live snapshots (customers by fuzzy name/postcode, services by name, staff by first name). Step 3 (Sonnet for multi-constraint verbs schedule.edit/optimise/explain/price; Haiku for parse-only verbs add/find/message-draft): produce structured patch JSON + reasoning + confidence. Step 4: render verb-specific diff modal (ScheduleDiffModal, CustomerDiffModal, ServiceDiffModal, InsightCard, MessageReviewTable). Step 5: Apply runs the existing mutation paths (updateJob, addCustomer, updateService) inside a transaction; failure rolls back and re-prompts. Telemetry: log {intent, confidence, applied, edited_before_apply} to tune prompts.

roadmap.feature: Conflict & risk detector (schedule, passive amber chip)
Smart recurrence inference on job create
Voice/text Quick Add ('Add Mrs Henderson 2pm Friday...')
Service description rewriter (Polish for Front Desk)
Cadi AI command bar (intent router + diff modals)
Auto-fill empty schedule slots from backlog
Service-menu gap detector from lost leads
Smart service bundling from invoice co-occurrence
Revenue gap analyser on Quarter view
Profitability X-ray (cross services + payroll + route + customers)
A/B testing layer for service descriptions on Front Desk
Sonnet nightly batches (churn, profit, insights, gap): ~1.5M tokens/user/mo cached
Sonnet interactive (command bar, schedule edits, pricing): ~800k tokens/user/mo
Total AI COGS target on £29/mo tier
